name: Editorial nursery realism
headline: Know what happened. Know what’s next.
subhead: One shared baby log that turns today’s care into the next nap window.
style: warm premium nursery editorial photography, cream paper, linen, wood, sage leaves, moon and sun props, realistic iPhone, soft but high-contrast typography, expensive parenting magazine feel
avoid: no cartoon mascots, no generic gradient poster, no paywall, no QR code, no fake awards
prompt:
Create a pure Nano Banana 2 App Store screenshot style exploration for BabyRelay.

INPUT: The image input is the real BabyRelay Today app screen. Preserve it as the product truth inside a modern iPhone frame; keep the app UI recognizable, readable, and honest.

OUTPUT: One complete 6.9-inch iPhone App Store portrait screenshot concept, roughly 9:19.5 aspect ratio.

HEADLINE TEXT (verbatim): "Know what happened. Know what’s next."
SUBHEAD TEXT (verbatim): "One shared baby log that turns today’s care into the next nap window."
STYLE DIRECTION: Editorial nursery realism — warm premium nursery editorial photography, cream paper, linen, wood, sage leaves, moon and sun props, realistic iPhone, soft but high-contrast typography, expensive parenting magazine feel.

PRODUCT STORY: BabyRelay is a shared baby care log. It lets caregivers log sleep, feeds, diapers, notes, and night wakes, then shows the next-up guidance and timeline for the selected child.

COMPOSITION: Large readable headline at the top, concise subhead below, realistic iPhone frame with the app UI as the hero in the middle/lower area. Add style-specific environmental details around the phone only. The screenshot should look like a polished App Store creative direction, not a mockup template.

STRICT CONSTRAINTS: no cartoon mascots, no generic gradient poster, no paywall, no QR code, no fake awards. Do not invent app features, fake metrics, fake subscription offers, ratings, awards, App Store badges, or medical/sleep-training claims. Do not include the paywall screen. Keep the in-phone product UI legible.
